result:
The tokenizer classifies a token using the `classify` function, which tests the raw string against regular expressions [1]:
- A token is classified as a `"number"` if it matches `/^\d+$/` (consisting only of digits) [1].
- A token is classified as a `"word"` if it matches `/^[A-Za-z]+$/` (consisting only of letters) [1].
- If it matches neither, it is classified as a `"symbol"` [1].
